title: New ransomware campaign targeting cloud infrastructure
description: Security researchers have identified a new ransomware variant specifically designed to target cloud-based infrastructure. The campaign uses compromised CI/CD pipelines to deploy malicious code.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The ransomware campaign targeting cloud infrastructure poses a significant threat to organizations relying on cloud services. By exploiting compromised CI/CD pipelines, attackers can deploy malicious code, potentially leading to data encryption, service disruption, and financial losses.
recommended_actions: Conduct an immediate review of CI/CD pipelines to identify and mitigate any unauthorized access or vulnerabilities.
Implement robust backup and recovery strategies, ensuring backups are stored securely and isolated from production environments.
Enhance monitoring and detection capabilities for unusual activity within cloud environments, focusing on CI/CD pipelines.

title: Show HN: Open-source vulnerability scanner
description: New tool automatically scans dependencies for known vulnerabilities and suggests patches. Gaining traction in the security community.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The open-source vulnerability scanner itself does not pose a direct threat, but its widespread use may lead to potential risks if the tool contains flaws, is misused, or becomes a target for attackers. Additionally, reliance on automated tools without proper validation could result in overlooked vulnerabilities.
recommended_actions: Conduct a thorough review of the tool's source code before implementation to ensure it does not introduce vulnerabilities.
Complement the tool's findings with manual validation and additional security measures to avoid over-reliance.
Monitor the tool's updates and community feedback for any reported issues or improvements.

title: ImageMagick: Heap Buffer Over-Read in distributed pixel cache server
description: An attacker who can connect to a magick -distribute-cache service can cause a heap buffer over-read in the server process.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-47166.

title: ImageMagick: Information Disclosure in distributed pixel cache server because it is not using a challenge–response authentication model
description: The distributed pixel cache was originally designed to operate without a challenge–response authentication model. However, given today’s heightened security expectations, we have changed our implementation.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-47165.

title: ImageMagick: Race Condition in distributed pixel cache server can result in file descriptor hijacking
description: An attacker who can connect to a magick -distribute-cache service can hijack a file descriptor in the server process when a race condition is met.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-46693.

title: ImageMagick: Heap Buffer Over-Write in distributed pixel cache server
description: An attacker who can connect to a `magick -distribute-cache` service can cause a heap buffer over-write in the server process.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows an attacker to exploit a heap buffer over-write in the ImageMagick distributed pixel cache server, potentially leading to denial of service (DoS), data corruption, or remote code execution depending on the attacker's capabilities.
recommended_actions: Immediately update ImageMagick to the latest patched version that addresses CVE-2026-46692.
Restrict access to the `magick -distribute-cache` service by implementing network-level access controls, such as firewalls or VPNs, to limit connections to trusted sources only.
Monitor server logs for any unusual or unauthorized connection attempts to the distributed pixel cache service and investigate anomalies promptly.

title: The Ditty – Responsive News Tickers, Sliders, and Lists plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to...
description: The Ditty – Responsive News Tickers, Sliders, and Lists plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to authorization bypass in all versions up to, and including, 3.1.65. This is due to the plugin not properly verifying that a user is authorized to perform an action. This makes it possible for unauthenticated attackers to retrieve the full item content of non-public Dittys — including drafts, pending, scheduled, and disabled entries — by enumerating integer post IDs against the ditty_init AJAX endpoint. ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity. CVE: CVE-2026-9011.

description: The Vedrixa Forms – User Registration Form, Signup Form & Drag & Drop Form Builder plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to authorization bypass in all versions up to, and including, 1.1.1. ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows authenticated attackers with subscriber-level access or higher to bypass authorization checks and manipulate form structures, potentially leading to data integrity issues, phishing attacks, or unauthorized data collection.

Restrict user roles and permissions to minimize exposure to subscriber-level accounts.
Monitor and audit form changes for any unauthorized modifications.

This makes it possible for unauthenticated attackers to overwrite or delete the internal notes (_mphb_booking_internal_notes) of any booking by supplying an arbitrary booking ID. The nonce for this action is output in the HTML source of every public page through wp_local

description: The FluentCRM – Email Newsletter, Automation, Email Marketing, Email Campaigns, Optins, Leads, and CRM Solution plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Blind Server-Side Request Forgery in all versions up to, and including, 2.9.87 via the 'SubscribeURL' parameter. Exploitation requires that the

title: The AudioIgniter plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Insecure Direct Object Reference in...
description: The AudioIgniter plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Insecure Direct Object Reference in versions up to, and including, 2.0.2. This is due to the handle_playlist_endpoint() function (hooked to template_redirect) accepting a user-controlled playlist ID via the audioigniter_playlist_id query var or the /audioigniter/playlist/{id}/ rewrite rule and returning playlist track data without performing any authentication, capability, or post_status check — only the post_type is validated. This makes it

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability in the AudioIgniter plugin allows unauthorized users to access sensitive playlist data by exploiting an insecure direct object reference (IDOR). This could lead to data leakage, unauthorized access to media assets, or further exploitation of the WordPress site.
affected_industries: Small and Medium Businesses using WordPress
recommended_actions: Immediately update the AudioIgniter plugin to the latest patched version if available. If no patch exists, disable the plugin temporarily.
Implement a web application firewall (WAF) to block unauthorized access to the vulnerable endpoint.
Review and monitor server logs for any suspicious activity related to the /audioigniter/playlist/{id}/ endpoint.

title:
A broken access
control vulnerability exists in the TeamViewer DEX Platform (On‑Premises) prior...
description:
A broken access
control vulnerability exists in the TeamViewer DEX Platform (On‑Premises) prior version 9.2. Certain backend API endpoints do not
correctly enforce authorization checks, allowing an authenticated user with low
privileges to perform actions and access resources intended only for higher‑privileged roles. An attacker with
low‑privileged credentials may exploit
this to gain unauthorized access to administrative or sensitive functionality.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-8381.

title: The Slider by Soliloquy – Responsive Image Slider for WordPress plugin for WordPress is...
description: The Slider by Soliloquy – Responsive Image Slider for WordPress plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Sensitive Information Exposure in all versions up to, and including, 2.8.1 via the map_meta_cap. This makes it possible for authenticated attackers, with subscriber-level access and above, to extract draft slider metadata including unpublished media URLs, captions, and slider configuration authored by administrators or editors.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-7636.

description: The Widget Context plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Cross-Site Request Forgery in all versions up to, and including, 1.3.3. This is due to missing or incorrect nonce validation on the save_widget_context_settings function. This makes it possible for unauthenticated attackers to modify widget visibility context settings stored in the WordPress options table via a forged POST request to /wp-admin/widgets.php via a forged request granted they can trick a site administrator into performing an a

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-7615.

title: The Easy Elements for Elementor – Addons & Website Templates plugin for WordPress is vulnerable...
description: The Easy Elements for Elementor – Addons & Website Templates plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Privilege Escalation in all versions up to, and including, 1.4.5 via the `easyel_handle_register()` function. This is due to the `wp_ajax_nopriv_eel_register` AJAX handler iterating the attacker-controlled `custom_meta` POST array and writing every supplied key-value pair to the newly created user's meta via `update_user_meta()` without any key whitelist or blocklist, allowing the `wp_capabilities`

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows attackers to escalate privileges by exploiting the lack of proper validation in the `easyel_handle_register()` function. This could lead to unauthorized access, data breaches, or full control of the WordPress site.
recommended_actions: Immediately update the Easy Elements for Elementor plugin to a patched version (if available) or temporarily disable the plugin until a fix is released.
Implement a Web Application Firewall (WAF) to block malicious POST requests targeting the `wp_ajax_nopriv_eel_register` endpoint.
Review and audit user accounts for unauthorized privilege escalations and remove any suspicious accounts or roles.

title: The WP Blockade plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Reflected Cross-Site Scripting via the ...
description: The WP Blockade plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Reflected Cross-Site Scripting via the 'shortcode' parameter in all versions up to and including 0.9.14. This is due to insufficient input sanitization and output escaping in the render_shortcode_preview() function. The function receives user input from $_GET['shortcode'], passes it through stripslashes() without any sanitization, and then outputs it directly via echo do_shortcode($shortcode) on line 393. When the input is not a valid WordPre

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows attackers to inject malicious scripts via the 'shortcode' parameter, potentially leading to data theft, session hijacking, or defacement of affected WordPress websites.
recommended_actions: Update the WP Blockade plugin to the latest patched version once available or disable the plugin temporarily.
Implement a web application firewall (WAF) to block malicious requests targeting the 'shortcode' parameter.
Audit WordPress plugins regularly for vulnerabilities and remove or replace outdated plugins.

title: The CBX 5 Star Rating & Review plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Reflected Cross-Site...
description: The CBX 5 Star Rating & Review plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Reflected Cross-Site Scripting via the 'page' parameter in all versions up to, and including, 1.0.7 due to insufficient input sanitization and output escaping. This makes it possible for unauthenticated attackers to inject arbitrary web scripts in pages that execute if they can successfully trick an administrator into performing an action such as clicking on a link.

This makes it possible for unauthenticated attackers to delete arbitrary plugin feed data (from alfie_colindex, alfie_producten, alfie_reactions, and alfie_searchproduct tables) via a forged request granted they can trick a site admini

title: An attacker sending tcp, il, rudp, rudp, or gre packets with a length less than the header size...
description: An attacker sending tcp, il, rudp, rudp, or gre packets with a length less than the header size would trigger a kernel panic.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows an attacker to send malformed packets (TCP, IL, RUDP, or GRE) with a length less than the header size, leading to a kernel panic. This could result in Denial of Service (DoS) attacks, causing system crashes and disruptions to critical operations.
recommended_actions: Immediately apply patches or updates provided by the vendor to address CVE-2026-9054.
Implement network-level filtering to block malformed packets with lengths less than the header size.
Enable logging and monitoring for unusual packet activity to detect potential exploitation attempts.

title: The KIA Subtitle plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Stored Cross-Site Scripting via the plugin...
description: The KIA Subtitle plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Stored Cross-Site Scripting via the plugin's `the-subtitle` shortcode `before` and `after` attributes in all versions up to, and including, 4.0.1. This is due to insufficient input sanitization and output escaping on user supplied attributes. This makes it possible for authenticated attackers, with Contributor-level access and above, to inject arbitrary web scripts in pages that will execute whenever a user accesses an injected page.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-7509.

title: Mothra would respect a default value given by a website for HTML file upload forms. An attacker...
description: Mothra would respect a default value given by a website for HTML file upload forms. An attacker could craft a website with a malicious default file path, and then conceal this form element.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows attackers to exploit HTML file upload forms by injecting a malicious default file path. This could lead to unauthorized file uploads, data exfiltration, or execution of malicious scripts, potentially compromising user data and system integrity.
recommended_actions: Validate and sanitize all user inputs, including file upload paths, on the server side to prevent exploitation of default values.
Implement Content Security Policy (CSP) headers to restrict the execution of malicious scripts and mitigate potential impacts.
Update the Mothra library or related dependencies to the latest patched version addressing CVE-2026-9053.

description: The Location Weather plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to unauthorized modification of data due to missing capability checks on the `splw_update_block_options()` and `lwp_clean_weather_transients()` functions in all versions up to, and including, 3.0.2. This makes it possible for authenticated attackers, with Contributor-level access and above, to disable all weather blocks and purge all weather cache transients. The nonce required for these actions is exposed to all authenticated users via `wp

title: CVE-2023-31317 - Improper restriction of operations within the bounds of a memory buffer in the AMD secure processer
description: Improper restriction of operations within the bounds of a memory buffer in the AMD secure processer (ASP) could allow an attacker to read or write to protected memory potentially resulting in arbitrary code execution.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability in the AMD secure processor could allow attackers to read or write to protected memory, potentially leading to arbitrary code execution. This could compromise sensitive data and system integrity, especially in environments relying on AMD processors for secure operations.
recommended_actions: Apply the latest security patches and firmware updates provided by AMD to mitigate the vulnerability.
Implement strict access controls and monitoring to detect and prevent unauthorized access to systems using AMD processors.
Conduct a thorough risk assessment of systems relying on AMD secure processors and prioritize patching in critical environments.

title: CVE-2026-8612 - WWW::Mechanize::Cached versions before 2.00 for Perl deserialize cached HTTP responses from a world-
description:
WWW::Mechanize::Cached versions before 2.00 for Perl deserialize cached HTTP responses from a world-writable on-disk cache, enabling local response forgery and code execution.

With no explicit cache backend, WWW::Mechanize::Cached constructs a default Cache::FileCache under /tmp/FileCache without overriding the backend's documented directory_umask of 000, so the cache root and its subdirectories are created mode 0777 with no sticky bit. Cache entries are named by sha1_hex of the request and read back through Storable::thaw on the next cache hit.

A local attacker with write access to the cache tree can replace a victim's cache entry for a known URL with an arbitrary frozen HTTP::Response blob, causing the victim's next get() of that URL to return attacker controlled response bytes. Because the bytes are passed to Storable::thaw, a victim process that has loaded any class with a side-effectful STORABLE_thaw, DESTROY, or overload hook can be escalated to arbitrary code execution.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-8612.

description: A System Management Mode (SMM) handler could perform a callout to code located in non-SMM/untrusted memory.
